context: Tests whether the agent uses pyxll.xl_app() to obtain the Excel Application object when writing PyXLL add-in code, rather than using win32com.client.Dispatch('Excel.Application') which is only appropriate for external scripts running outside Excel.

checklist.name: xl_app imported from pyxll
checklist.description: Code imports xl_app from the pyxll package, e.g. 'from pyxll import xl_app' or 'import pyxll' followed by 'pyxll.xl_app'

checklist.name: xl_app() called in @xl_func
checklist.description: Inside the worksheet function body, xl_app() is called to get the Application object (not assigned at module level)

checklist.name: xl_app() called in @xl_menu
checklist.description: Inside the menu action function body, xl_app() is called to get the Application object

checklist.name: No Dispatch in PyXLL functions
checklist.description: Code does NOT use win32com.client.Dispatch('Excel.Application') inside either PyXLL-decorated function

checklist.name: @xl_func decorator used
checklist.description: The active-sheet-name function is decorated with @xl_func

checklist.name: @xl_menu decorator used
checklist.description: The CSV loader function is decorated with @xl_menu

checklist.name: xl_app() inside function body
checklist.description: xl_app() is called inside the function body, not at module level (module-level calls fail when the module is imported outside Excel)

checklist.name: Active worksheet accessed via app
checklist.description: Active worksheet is accessed via the app object returned by xl_app(), e.g. app.ActiveSheet or app.ActiveWorkbook.ActiveSheet
